subject: Database replication lag causing stale portfolio data
description:
Hi Data Platform team,

We've noticed that the portfolio valuations dashboard is showing data that's
approximately 45 minutes stale. The replication from the primary SQL Server
(SQLPROD-NYC-01) to the read replica (SQLREPLICA-NYC-03) appears to be lagging.

Impact: Portfolio managers are making decisions based on outdated valuations.
This affects approximately 30 PMs across Wealth Management and Asset Management.

What we've confirmed so far:
- Primary database is current (checked via direct query at 14:22 ET)
- Read replica is behind by ~2,700 transactions
- The lag started around 13:35 ET today
- No recent schema changes or maintenance windows
- Disk I/O on the replica server looks elevated: avg write latency 48ms vs
  normal 5ms

We need this resolved urgently. The EOD NAV calculation process kicks off at
16:00 ET and reads from the replica. If the lag isn't cleared by then, we'll
have incorrect NAV calculations which will trigger regulatory reporting
discrepancies and we'll need to file correc

description:
Hi IT Support,

Forwarding the full infrastructure review notes below — my actual issue is buried
near the bottom. Please scroll down.

====== QUARTERLY IT INFRASTRUCTURE REVIEW — Q1 2026 ======
Date: March 14, 2026 | Attendees: IT Steering Committee
Location: Building 4, Executive Conference Room 4A

AGENDA ITEM 1: Cloud Migration Status
- Azure landing zone provisioning is 94% complete across all three regions.
- Remaining workloads: legacy COBOL settlement engine (target: May 2026),
  on-prem Oracle RAC cluster for derivatives pricing (target: July 2026).
- Cost optimization: reserved instance coverage improved from 61% to 78%.
- Action: Cloud team to finalize RI recommendations for Q2 by March 28.

AGENDA ITEM 2: Endpoint Refresh Program
- 1,200 of 3,400 laptops refreshed to Dell Latitude 5550 with Windows 11 23H2.
- Remaining rollout schedule: NYC Building 1-3 (April), London (May), Singapore (June).
- Dock compatibility issues reported with TB16 docks — WD19S replacement in progress.
- BitLocker recovery key escrow migration to Intune on track for April 1.
- Action: Endpoint team to publish refresh FAQ on the intranet by March 21.

AGENDA ITEM 3: Network Capacity Planning
- Core switch fabric utilization peaked at 72% during February month-end processing.
- Wireless controller firmware upgraded to 8.10.185 across all NYC APs.
- SD-WAN deployment for London and Singapore branch offices approved — RFP in April.
- Action: Network ops to model 100G uplink requirements for 2027 budget.

AGENDA ITEM 4: Security & Compliance Update
- SOC 2 Type II audit fieldwork begins April 7. All evidence must be staged by April 1.
- Phishing simulation click rate dropped from 12% to 8.5% — goal is 5% by year-end.
- Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) rollout to Tier 0 admins complete.
- Action: Security ops to deliver PAW Tier 1 rollout plan by March 28.

AGENDA ITEM 5: Service Desk Metrics
- Mean time to resolve (MTTR): 4.2 hours (target 4.0) — slight regression from Feb.
- First-contact resolution rate: 68% (target 70%).
- Top ticket categories: Password resets (24%), VPN issues (18%), Outlook (14%).
- Action: Evaluate chatbot deflection for password reset tickets.

AGENDA ITEM 6: Disaster Recovery Testing
- Full DR failover test for Azure-hosted trading platform scheduled for April 19-20.
- RPO target: 15 minutes. RTO target: 2 hours. Last test achieved RPO 12m, RTO 1h 48m.
- Cross-region replication lag for Cosmos DB reduced from 320ms to 95ms.
- Action: DR team to coordinate with Trading and Compliance for the test window.

AGENDA ITEM 7: Data Center Decommissioning
- Secaucus DC lease expires September 2026. 14 remaining racks to be migrated.
- Physical server inventory audit complete — 6 servers unaccounted for, investigating.
- Tape backup archive migration to Azure Blob Archive tier: 340TB of 480TB complete.
- Action: Facilities to confirm decommissioning timeline with vendor by April 4.

--- END OF AGENDA ITEMS ---

** MY ACTUAL ISSUE (sorry for the long email) **

On page 2 of the notes it mentions TB16 dock compatibility issues. I'm one of the
affected users — my Dell Latitude 5550 with the TB16 dock loses USB-C video output
intermittently. Both my external Dell U2722D monitors go black for 5-10 seconds
every 20-30 minutes. I'm on the 8th floor, Building 4. The old WD19S dock I had
before worked perfectly but it was collected during the refresh.

Thanks,
Jonathan Park

subject: Production report query keeps timing out
description:
Hi team,

My daily P&L reconciliation report has been timing out since Monday morning. The query runs against the production warehouse and normally finishes in about 2 minutes, but now it just hangs until the 30-minute timeout kills it. Here is the query I'm running:

SELECT
    t.trade_id,
    t.trade_date,
    t.settlement_date,
    t.counterparty_id,
    cp.counterparty_name,
    cp.lei_code,
    t.instrument_type,
    t.isin,
    sec.security_description,
    sec.currency_code,
    t.quantity,
    t.price,
    t.notional_amount,
    t.accrued_interest,
    t.net_settlement_amount,
    t.trade_status,
    pos.start_of_day_position,
    pos.end_of_day_position,
    pos.realized_pnl,
    pos.unrealized_pnl,
    pos.total_pnl,
    fx.exchange_rate,
    fx.rate_source,
    CASE
        WHEN t.notional_amount > 10000000 THEN 'LARGE'
        WHEN t.notional_amount > 1000000 THEN 'MEDIUM'
        ELSE 'SMALL'
    END AS trade_size_bucket,
    COALESCE(r.regulatory_flag, 'NONE') AS reg_flag
FROM trades t
INNER JOIN counterparties cp ON t.counterparty_id = cp.counterparty_id
INNER JOIN securities sec ON t.isin = sec.isin
LEFT JOIN positions pos ON t.trade_id = pos.trade_id
    AND pos.position_date = t.trade_date
LEFT JOIN fx_rates fx ON sec.currency_code = fx.currency_code
    AND fx.rate_date = t.trade_date
LEFT JOIN regulatory_flags r ON t.trade_id = r.trade_id
WHERE t.trade_date BETWEEN '2026-03-01' AND '2026-03-17'
    AND t.trade_status IN ('CONFIRMED', 'SETTLED', 'PENDING')
    AND t.book_id IN (
        SELECT book_id FROM book_permissions
        WHERE analyst_id = 'HWONG'
    )
ORDER BY t.trade_date DESC, t.notional_amount DESC;

Nothing changed on my end — same query I run every day. I suspect something happened to the database indexes over the weekend. Can someone from the data platform team investigate?

Thanks,
Henry Wong
Portfolio Analytics, Building 4

subject: Teams integration not syncing with calendar
description:
# Issue Report: Teams Calendar Integration Failure

**Reporter:** Amara Osei
**Department:** Client Relations
**Date:** 2026-03-17

## Problem Description

The Microsoft Teams integration with my Outlook calendar has completely stopped working. When I schedule a meeting in **Outlook**, it *does not* appear in **Teams**, and vice versa. This started after the IT department pushed the latest `Teams Desktop Client v24.7.1` update last Thursday.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Outlook Desktop** and create a new meeting
2. Add a Teams link by clicking `Add Teams Meeting`
3. Send the invite
4. Open **Microsoft Teams** > Calendar tab
5. Notice the meeting **does not appear**

## What I've Tried

- [x] Signed out and back into Teams
- [x] Cleared the Teams cache (`%appdata%\Microsoft\Teams\Cache`)
- [x] Restarted my laptop
- [ ] Reinstalled Teams (waiting for IT approval)

## Expected Behavior

Meetings created in Outlook should appear in Teams calendar within ~30 seconds, as documented in the [Teams admin guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/exchange-teams-interact).

## Impact

I have **5+ client meetings daily** and have already missed joining two calls because they didn't show in Teams. This is affecting client experience.

---

```
Teams Version: 24.7.1.0
Outlook Version: 16.0.17328.20162
OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 23H2
```

Please advise. Thanks!

description:
Our integration with the compliance reporting API is failing. The API returns an error response, but the error body contains triple-escaped JSON that's nearly impossible to read. Here's the raw response:

{"status":"error","code":500,"message":"Internal processing failure","details":"{\"pipeline\":\"compliance-etl-v2\",\"stage\":\"transform\",\"error\":{\"type\":\"SchemaValidationError\",\"message\":\"Field \\\\\\\"transaction_id\\\\\\\" expected type \\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\" but received \\\\\\\"null\\\\\\\"\",\"context\":{\"record_number\":45892,\"batch_id\":\"BATCH-20260317-0923\",\"source_table\":\"dbo.RawTransactions\",\"target_schema\":\"{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fields\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":[{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"name\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"transaction_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}]}\"}}}"

The actual issue is that null transaction_id values are getting through from the RawTransactions table in batch BATCH-20260317-0923, which started at 9:23 AM today. This is breaking the compliance ETL pipeline and we can't generate the daily regulatory report.

The pipeline runs on our Azure Data Factory instance (adf-contoso-prod-eastus2). We need this fixed before the 5 PM regulatory filing deadline.

James O'Brien
Regulatory Affairs

subject: Container deployment failing in staging
description:
Hi team,

Our staging deployment pipeline is failing. The risk-engine container keeps crash-looping. Here's the docker-compose.yml we're using:

```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  risk-engine:
    image: contoso.azurecr.io/risk-engine:2.4.1
    deploy:
      replicas: 3
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '4'
          memory: 8G
        reservations:
          cpus: '2'
          memory: 4G
    environment:
      - REDIS_HOST=redis-cluster.staging.svc
      - REDIS_PORT=6379
      - DB_HOST=sqlserver-staging.contoso.internal
      - DB_PORT=1433
      - DB_NAME=RiskCalcStaging
      - LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
      - MARKET_DATA_ENDPOINT=http://market-data:8080/api/v2
    ports:
      - '8443:8443'
    depends_on:
      - redis-cluster
      - market-data
    healthcheck:
      test: ['CMD', 'curl', '-f', 'http://localhost:8443/health']
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
  redis-cluster:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - '6379:6379'
  market-data:
    image: contoso.azurecr.io/market-data-mock:1.0.0
    ports:
      - '8080:8080'
```

The container logs show:
```
risk-engine_1  | 2026-03-17 08:15:03 ERROR Failed to connect to sqlserver-staging.contoso.internal:1433 — Connection refused
risk-engine_1  | 2026-03-17 08:15:03 ERROR Database initialization failed after 5 retries
risk-engine_1  | 2026-03-17 08:15:03 FATAL Shutting down due to unrecoverable error
```

It looks like the SQL Server staging instance is unreachable from the container. The database was migrated to a new server last week — maybe the hostname changed?

subject: Code review system broken after merge
description:
The PR review system stopped working after my merge. Here's the diff that broke it:

```diff
diff --git a/src/auth/middleware.py b/src/auth/middleware.py
index 7a8b9c0..1d2e3f4 100644
--- a/src/auth/middleware.py
+++ b/src/auth/middleware.py
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ class AuthMiddleware:
     def validate_token(self, token: str) -> bool:
-        if not token or len(token) < 10:
+        if not token:
             return False
-        return self._verify_signature(token)
+        # TODO: re-enable signature verification after cert rotation
+        return True  # TEMPORARY BYPASS
 
     def _verify_signature(self, token: str) -> bool:
         try:
@@ -67,3 +68,8 @@ class AuthMiddleware:
             logger.error(f'Token verification failed: {e}')
             return False
+
+    def _skip_auth_for_testing(self):
+        # Added for load testing - remove before production

```

After this merge, the Azure DevOps pipeline that runs PR checks started returning 500 errors on the webhook endpoint. The pipeline was working fine before the merge. I don't think my code changes caused the pipeline failure — it's more likely something with the DevOps agent or the webhook configuration.

Can someone check the pipeline agent pool and the webhook endpoint?

subject: Report aggregation returning wrong totals
description:
Hi Data Platform team,

The quarterly revenue report is showing incorrect aggregation totals. Here is the query I am running:

WITH regional_sales AS (
    SELECT r.region_name, SUM(s.amount) AS total_sales
    FROM sales s
    JOIN regions r ON s.region_id = r.id
    WHERE s.sale_date BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-03-31'
    GROUP BY r.region_name
),
top_regions AS (
    SELECT region_name, total_sales
    FROM regional_sales
    WHERE total_sales > (
        SELECT SUM(total_sales) / 10 FROM regional_sales
    )
)
SELECT o.order_id, o.amount, t.region_name, t.total_sales
FROM orders o
JOIN top_regions t ON o.region_name = t.region_name
ORDER BY t.total_sales DESC;

The total_sales column does not match what Finance is reporting. I think the JOIN between orders and top_regions is creating duplicates but I am not sure. Can someone take a look?

Thanks,
Raj Patel
Data Engineering

subject: Bloomberg data feed mapping error — CUSIP/ISIN mismatch
description:
Hi Enterprise Apps,

We are seeing incorrect data in the Bloomberg terminal for several securities. The mapping between identifiers is wrong:

- AAPL US Equity (BBG ticker) maps to CUSIP 037833100 which is correct
- But MSFT US Equity maps to CUSIP 594918104 which is actually the CUSIP for META
- ISIN US5949181045 should be MSFT but resolves to META in our system
- SEDOL 2588173 (MSFT on LSE) maps correctly in London but not NY
- BBG FIGI BBG000BPH459 shows MSFT but pulls META pricing

This started after the overnight data feed refresh at 02:15 UTC. The raw feed file looks correct but something in our mapping layer is swapping MSFT and META identifiers.

This is impacting P&L calculations for the equities desk.

Thanks,
Victor Nwosu
Quantitative Analysis

=== docker-compose.yml (base) ===

```mermaid
    A[Trade Executed] --> B{Validation OK?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Send to Matching Engine]
    B -->|No| D[Return to Trader]
    C --> E{Match Found?}
    E -->|Yes| F[Generate Confirmation]
    E -->|No| G[Queue for Manual Match]
    F --> H[Counterparty Ack]
    H --> I{Ack Received?}
    I -->|Yes| J[Schedule Settlement]
    I -->|No, timeout 4hr| K[Escalate to Ops]
    J --> L[Settlement Complete]
    K --> M[Manual Resolution]
```

The process appears to be stuck between step F (Generate Confirmation) and step H (Counterparty Ack). The confirmations ARE being generated — I can see them in the Confirmations table — but the SWIFT gateway does not appear to be transmitting them to the counterparties.

Here is the sequence as I understand it:

```plantuml
@startuml
participant SettleFlow
participant ConfirmationSvc
participant SWIFTGateway
participant Counterparty

SettleFlow -> ConfirmationSvc: generateConfirm(tradeId)
ConfirmationSvc --> SettleFlow: confirmId=CFM-20240315-001
SettleFlow -> SWIFTGateway: send(confirmId)  <-- THIS TIMES OUT
SWIFTGateway --> SettleFlow: timeout after 120s
SettleFlow -> SettleFlow: retry (max 3)
SWIFTGateway --> SettleFlow: timeout after 120s
SettleFlow -> SettleFlow: mark as PENDING_CONFIRMATION
@enduml
```

There are currently 142 trades stuck in this state. The operations team is manually calling counterparties to confirm, but this is not sustainable. Can someone look at the SWIFT gateway connectivity?

Priya Venkatesh
Settlement Operations Analyst
Post-Trade Services
Desk: 3-East, Row C

subject: ContosoCalc option pricing module returns wrong Greeks — formula ref
description:
Hi Support,

The ContosoCalc pricing library (v8.3.1) is returning incorrect Delta and Gamma values for European-style equity options. I've been comparing the output against our validated reference spreadsheet and the values diverge significantly for deep in-the-money options.

For reference, the correct Black-Scholes formulas that ContosoCalc should be implementing are:

The option price:
$$C = S_0 N(d_1) - K e^{-rT} N(d_2)$$

Where:
$$d_1 = \frac{\ln(S_0/K) + (r + \sigma^2/2)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}}$$
$$d_2 = d_1 - \sigma\sqrt{T}$$

Delta should be:
$$\Delta = \frac{\partial C}{\partial S} = N(d_1)$$

And Gamma:
$$\Gamma = \frac{\partial^2 C}{\partial S^2} = \frac{N'(d_1)}{S_0 \sigma \sqrt{T}}$$

Where $N'(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}} e^{-x^2/2}$ is the standard normal PDF.

The specific test case where I see the error:
- Spot price $S_0 = 150$, Strike $K = 100$, Risk-free rate $r = 0.05$
- Volatility $\sigma = 0.20$, Time to expiry $T = 0.5$ years
- Expected Delta: 0.9782, ContosoCalc returns: 0.8431
- Expected Gamma: 0.0045, ContosoCalc returns: 0.0312

The error seems to be in the $d_1$ calculation — I suspect the library is using $\sigma$ instead of $\sigma^2/2$ in the drift term, which would be a classic implementation bug:
$$d_1^{\text{buggy}} = \frac{\ln(S_0/K) + (r + \sigma)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}} \neq d_1^{\text{correct}}$$

This was introduced in v8.3.1 — version 8.3.0 was correct. Can the quantitative development team investigate?

Natalie Zhao
Quantitative Analyst
Derivatives Pricing
Floor 12, Quant Lab

subject: CI/CD pipeline broken after branch merge — conflict markers in config
description:
Hi team,

Our CI/CD pipeline for the ContosoRisk service has been failing since a bad merge this morning. The build step fails because there are merge conflict markers left in the configuration file. Here's what the pipeline shows:

```
Step 4/12: Validate configuration
ERROR: Parse error in config/appsettings.Production.json at line 47
--- begin file excerpt ---
  "ConnectionStrings": {
<<<<<<< HEAD
    "RiskDatabase": "Server=risk-db-prod-01.database.windows.net;Database=contoso_risk;Encrypt=true;TrustServerCertificate=false;",
    "CacheConnection": "contoso-risk-redis.redis.cache.windows.net:6380,password=****,ssl=True,abortConnect=False"
=======
    "RiskDatabase": "Server=risk-db-prod-02.database.windows.net;Database=contoso_risk_v2;Encrypt=true;TrustServerCertificate=false;",
    "CacheConnection": "contoso-risk-redis-v2.redis.cache.windows.net:6380,password=****,ssl=True,abortConnect=False"
>>>>>>> feature/risk-engine-v2
  },
  "Logging": {
<<<<<<< HEAD
    "LogLevel": {
      "Default": "Warning",
      "Microsoft": "Warning"
    }
=======
    "LogLevel": {
      "Default": "Information",
      "Microsoft": "Warning",
      "ContosoRisk.Engine": "Debug"
    }
>>>>>>> feature/risk-engine-v2
  }
--- end file excerpt ---
```

The merge was between `main` and `feature/risk-engine-v2`. It looks like someone resolved the merge on GitHub but missed these conflict markers in the config file. The pipeline has been red for 4 hours now and the risk engine team can't deploy their hotfix.

Can someone help resolve the merge conflicts and get the pipeline green again?

Thanks,
Alex Rivera
DevOps

subject: Kubernetes pod crash loop — deployment config attached
description:
Hi team,

The ContosoSettlement service pods have been in CrashLoopBackOff since 5:30 AM this morning. Here's the deployment YAML for reference:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: contoso-settlement
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: contoso-settlement
    team: settlements
    version: v3.2.1
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: contoso-settlement
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: contoso-settlement
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: settlement-api
        image: contoso.azurecr.io/settlement-api:v3.2.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: '512Mi'
            cpu: '250m'
          limits:
            memory: '1Gi'
            cpu: '500m'
        env:
        - name: DB_HOST
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: settlement-db-secret
              key: host
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: settlement-db-secret
              key: password
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5

The pod logs show:
'FATAL: Could not connect to database at settlement-db-secret:host. Error: ECONNREFUSED 10.0.4.52:5432. Retries exhausted (5/5). Exiting.'

The database was migrated to a new IP on Friday (10.0.4.80) but the Kubernetes secret still points to the old IP. Can you update the secret and restart the pods?

Thanks,
Henry Park
Settlements
